title: LCI resources and payload schemas diverge across spec 17.5 / Zephyr / Python / Rust
description: Spec 17.5 is implemented conformantly only by new Zephyr modules (coap_status.c, coap_keys.c, coap_config.c). Divergences: (1) Python /status returns {iid,pubkey,state,peers,gradients,announce_seq,uptime,firmware} (node.py:539-561) sharing ZERO keys with the spec schema {uptime_s,battery_pct,battery_mv,mem_free_kb,time,dodag,radio} that Zephyr lichen_coap_encode_status_cbor implements; Python /status is NOT observable though spec marks obs. (2) Python /config={receive_timeout_ms,announce_interval_ms} vs spec {name,role,radio,identity} (Zephyr conforms). (3) Python has no /keys, /status/neighbors, /status/routes, /config/radio, /config/identity (has legacy /key, /neighbors). (4) Rust node has no LCI-grade resources at all: /config is JSON text {"interval":60} with no CBOR content-format (dispatch.rs:312-313), /sensors placeholder JSON (not SenML), .well-known/core lacks rt/ct attributes; Rust gateway exposes none. (5) rt attributes: spec rt="status"/"config" vs Python/Zephyr-old rt="lichen.status" etc.; Zephyr-new exports no discovery metadata. (6) Python /proxy exists (with SSRF guard) but not in Zephyr/Rust. /key payload IS in parity (Python resources.py:811-828 == Zephyr-old coap_server.c:271-329). Error codes for shared subset consistent (4.00/2.04/2.02/4.04). DECISION NEEDED: spec 17.5 calls itself the authoritative native contract, making Python/Rust non-conformant - but changing Python breaks client/lci.py and the TUI. Converge schemas + resource sets, or explicitly version the contract. Relates f4xg.4 (proxy deviation only).
